name: Urban Upbound Federal Credit Union
description_short: Federal credit union serving public housing residents in NYC with affordable personal/business loans, financial counseling, and employment services focused on economic mobility.
description_long:
Urban Upbound Federal Credit Union (UUFCU) is a mission-driven credit union based in Queens, New York, with a specific focus on serving residents of public housing neighborhoods. Founded with the explicit mission to provide residents of public housing the tools and resources needed to achieve economic mobility and self-sufficiency, UUFCU operates as both a financial institution and a community development organization.

UUFCU offers core banking and lending products including personal loans at 4.99% APR, small business loans at 9.90% APR, bill pay services, money orders, direct deposit, and NYCHA rent payment processing. Members can open accounts with just $10 and access the COOP ATM network. The credit union provides free financial counseling from certified counselors and supports tax refund direct deposit for faster IRS refunds.

What distinguishes UUFCU is its integrated approach combining financial services with comprehensive employment and community services. Beyond traditional credit union products, they operate employment programs serving 2,000 adults and 3,000 youth annually, providing job readiness training, vocational preparation, career counseling, and placement services. They also offer financial counseling, income support services, tax preparation, and community revitalization initiatives—all designed specifically for public housing residents and underserved communities.

UUFCU operates two physical branches in Queens (Long Island City and Astoria) with a third Far Rockaway location coming soon, plus additional employment service centers in East Harlem and Astoria. While their loan rates are competitive and their mission is genuine, members should note limited branch availability compared to traditional banks, and services extend beyond typical credit union offerings into employment and social services, which may or may not align with individual needs.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Very low membership barrier—accounts open with just $10
Competitive personal loan rates at 4.99% APR
Free financial counseling from certified counselors included
Integrated employment services—2,000 adults and 3,000 youth served annually with job training and placement
Specialized NYCHA rent payment processing for public housing residents
Access to COOP ATM network for nationwide cash access
Mission-driven organization explicitly focused on breaking poverty cycles and economic mobility
cons: Limited branch availability—only 2 operating locations in Queens, with limited hours (Long Island City closes at 3pm weekdays)
Small business loan rates higher at 9.90% APR compared to many traditional lenders
Geographic focus on NYC public housing may limit relevance for non-residents or those outside target neighborhoods
Employment and community services may distract from core banking operations or create mission creep
No mention of online account opening, mobile banking features, or digital-first capabilities on website
best_for: Public housing residents in Queens seeking affordable personal loans and basic banking with community support
Low-income adults and youth in NYC seeking employment services combined with financial access
NYCHA residents who need specialized rent payment processing and financial counseling services
Small business owners in underserved communities seeking sub-10% lending with community mission alignment
diagnosis: Best for low-income residents of NYC public housing who need affordable lending combined with employment assistance and financial counseling. Primary caveat: very limited physical footprint (only 2 operating branches in Queens) and primary mission focus on public housing residents may limit accessibility for other consumers seeking traditional credit union services.
services: Free financial counseling from certified counselors
Adult employment services and job placement
Youth career pathways and training programs
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
